title: campanula diversifolia var. pusilla
intro: Campanula diversifolia var. pusilla, also known as the alpine campanula, is a small, flowering plant species within the bellflower family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The alpine campanula is believed to offer various health benefits, including antioxidant and antiseptic effects, which may help soothe skin irritations and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion, tincture, or dried herb, and its leaves and flowers are commonly consumed or applied topically.
